top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Alpi del Mare's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
Granfondo Alpi del Mare's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
68 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
7,451ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
108ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Granfondo Alpi del Mare, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Alpi del Mare, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Granfondo Alpi del Mare. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Alpi del Mare. Train it until it feels boring.
7,451ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 7,451ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: September in Mondovì, Piedmont, Italy. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Granfondo Alpi del Mare — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Alpi del Mare’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Alpi del Mare. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo Alpi del Mare requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and Durability to prepare you for 68 miles of mixed terrain in Mondovì, Piedmont, Italy.
